Exploration complete. Here is the full picture of the repository.

## 1. Directory tree (excluding node_modules, .git) with file sizes

```
workspace/
├── AGENTS.md                                   290 B
├── README.md                                   280 B
├── package.json                                226 B
├── tests-before.log                            930 B
├── data/
│   └── collections.json                       4366 B
├── migrations/
│   └── 001_collection_contract.mongodb.js      400 B
├── schema/
│   ├── access-paths.md                         761 B
│   └── schema.md                              1120 B
├── src/
│   ├── workflow.mjs                            207 B   <- the file to implement
│   ├── portal-view.mjs                        1053 B
│   └── render-proof.mjs                       2314 B
└── tests/
    └── acceptance.test.mjs                    2895 B
```

No `node_modules`, no lockfile, no `.env`, no config files. This is a pure ESM Node.js project with **zero dependencies** — no mongodb driver is installed or used.

## 2. package.json

```json
{
  "name": "ast-bench-invoice-dispute-workflow-mongo",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "node tests/acceptance.test.mjs",
    "render": "node src/render-proof.mjs"
  }
}
```

No dependencies/devDependencies. `"type": "module"` (ESM). Tests run via plain `node`, no test framework.

## 3. Test file (tests/acceptance.test.mjs) — full contents

This is the contract that must pass. Uses `node:assert`, reads `data/collections.json`, and imports `applyBenchmarkTask` from `src/workflow.mjs` and `buildPortalView` from `src/portal-view.mjs`.

Key assertions:
- **Before state** (unmodified data through `buildPortalView`): `status === "Needs work"`, `history === "Not visible"`, `tasks === "0 owner tasks"`.
- **After state** (`applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)` where `now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z"`):
  - `after.status === "Invoice dispute active with finance owner, evidence bundle, and customer-safe status."`
  - `after.owner === expectedOwners.join(" + ")` where `expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups` = `"Finance + Customer Success + Legal + Operations"`
  - `after.nextStep.length > 8`
  - `after.history === "Audit visible"`
  - `after.customerMessage.length > 12`
  - `after.riskSummary === "${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${names.join(", ")}"` = `"6 signals: invoice, dispute, workflow, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`
  - `after.tasks === "${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks"` = `"4 owner tasks"`
- **Persisted-state assertions** (`assertPersistedState`) — `applyBenchmarkTask` must MUTATE `runData` in place:
  - `db.workflow_state` must contain a doc with `requestId === request._id` whose `status` equals expectedStatus and whose `riskSignals` (mapped to `.name`) deep-equal expected signal names.
  - `db.owner_tasks` must contain exactly `expectedOwners.length` (4) docs with `requestId === request._id`; their `ownerGroup` values must deep-equal `expectedOwners` (order matters: Finance, Customer Success, Legal, Operations); every task must have truthy `title`, truthy `dueAt`, and `status === "open"`.
  - `db.customer_messages` must contain a doc with matching `requestId` and truthy `body`.
  - `db.audit_events` must contain a doc with matching `requestId` and `customerVisible` truthy.

The full assertions reference `request._id` which is `"invoice-invoice-disp"` (note: NOT the same as `request.taskId`).

## 4. Source files — full contents

**src/workflow.mjs** (the stub to implement):
```js
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  // TODO: Implement the benchmark workflow against the native data shape.
  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

**src/portal-view.mjs** (the read projection — DO NOT change; tests depend on it as-is):
```js
export function buildPortalView(db) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

  return {
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

This reveals exactly the persisted shapes `applyBenchmarkTask` must write:
- `workflow_state` doc: `{ requestId, title, status, nextStep, riskSignals: [{name,...}] }`
- `owner_tasks` docs: `{ requestId, ownerGroup, title, dueAt, status: "open" }`
- `audit_events` doc: `{ requestId, customerVisible: true, ... }`
- `customer_messages` doc: `{ requestId, body }`

**src/render-proof.mjs** (rendering script for `npm run render`, writes `artifacts/before-after.json` and `.svg`; not part of test assertions). Full contents read — it imports both `applyBenchmarkTask` and `buildPortalView`, computes before/after, and renders a comparison SVG. No changes needed here.

## 5. Fixture/seed data (data/collections.json)

A single JSON object (not separate files) holding all "collections" as arrays. Structure and key first records:

- `benchmark_fixture`: object `{ _id, suiteId, taskId, generatedAt }`
- `accounts`: 1 doc — `{ _id: "acct-invoice-di", name, tier: "enterprise", region, contract{...}, contacts[...], context{ healthScore, usageTrend, openCases, invoiceRisk, complianceFlags:["customer-visible-audit"] } }`
- `workflow_requests`: 1 doc — **the driver record**:
  - `_id: "invoice-invoice-disp"`, `taskId: "invoice-dispute-workflow"`, `accountId: "acct-invoice-di"`, `title: "Invoice dispute workflow"`, `primaryEntity: "invoice"`
  - `expectedOutcome: "Invoice dispute active with finance owner, evidence bundle, and customer-safe status."`
  - `ownerGroups: ["Finance", "Customer Success", "Legal", "Operations"]` (4)
  - `riskSignals`: 6 objects, each `{ name, detail }` — names: `invoice, dispute, workflow, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required`
  - `nextStep: "Finance owner review by 16:00"`
  - `customerMessage: "Invoice dispute workflow is being handled by Finance, Customer Success, Legal, Operations."`
- `activities`: 6 docs — `{ _id, accountId, subjectId, summary, occurredAt }`
- `workflow_state`: `[]` (empty — to be populated)
- `owner_tasks`: `[]` (empty — to be populated)
- `audit_events`: `[]` (empty — to be populated)
- `customer_messages`: `[]` (empty — to be populated)

Note: `expectedStatus` in the test exactly matches `workflow_requests[0].expectedOutcome`. The request already carries `nextStep` and `customerMessage` values you can reuse.

## 6. README / task description files

- **README.md**: States this is a frozen AST-Bench target workspace; the workflow is intentionally missing; the acceptance test must fail before implementation. Commands: `npm test`, `npm run render`.
- **AGENTS.md**: Target rules — Implement only the invoice dispute workflow; use existing document-shaped collections in `data/`; do NOT change tests; do NOT change generated fixture data; run `npm test` before finishing; keep behavior-equivalent to the acceptance contract.
- **schema/schema.md**: Business prompt and schema contract (lane: mongo). Lists object field shapes. Confirms `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages` start at 0 documents.
- **schema/access-paths.md**: Describes read path (load request → reconstruct context → resolve owner groups & risk signals → persist state/tasks/message/audit → render portal projection) and write path (persist 1 workflow state, all owner tasks, 1 customer-safe message, 1 customer-visible audit event). Fairness rule: lane may stay DB-native but must not change tests or fixture data.

## 7. Database connection setup

**There is no live database connection.** Despite the "mongo" lane name and the MongoDB MCP server being available in this environment, the actual code operates on a plain in-memory JS object parsed from `data/collections.json`. The "collections" are JS arrays; `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` receives that object and must mutate its arrays in place. No `mongodb` driver, no connection string, no `MongoClient` anywhere. `migrations/001_collection_contract.mongodb.js` is just a `db.createCollection(...)` script (documentation/contract artifact), not executed by the tests.

## 8. Existing models/schemas

No ORM/ODM models (no Mongoose). The implicit schema is defined by:
- `migrations/001_collection_contract.mongodb.js` — declares 7 collections: `benchmark_fixture, accounts, workflow_requests, activities, workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages`.
- `schema/schema.md` — field-level shapes per collection.
- `src/portal-view.mjs` — the de facto schema for the four output collections (the exact field names the reader expects).

---

**Summary:** This is a zero-dependency ESM Node.js benchmark target where the only file to implement is `src/workflow.mjs`'s `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)`, which must mutate the in-memory `db` object (arrays from `data/collections.json`) to persist one `workflow_state`, four `owner_tasks` (ownerGroups in order, each with title/dueAt/status:"open"), one `customer_messages.body`, and one customer-visible `audit_events` entry keyed by `requestId === "invoice-invoice-disp"`, then return the portal view — no real MongoDB connection is involved despite the "mongo" lane name.
